dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMiMo-V2.5-ASR\u003c/strong\u003e is a state-of-the-art end-to-end automatic speech recognition (ASR) model developed by the Xiaomi MiMo team. It is built to deliver accurate and robust transcription across Mandarin Chinese and English, multiple Chinese dialects, code-switched speech, song lyrics, knowledge-intensive content, noisy acoustic environments, and multi-speaker conversations. MiMo-V2.5-ASR achieves state-of-the-art results on a wide range of public benchmarks.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eAbstract\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-abstract\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Abstract\" href=\"#abstract\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAutomatic speech recognition systems are expected to faithfully transcribe speech signals that originate from diverse languages, dialects, accents, and domains, and that are captured under a wide variety of acoustic conditions. While conventional end-to-end models perform well on in-domain data, they still fall short of real-world requirements in challenging scenarios such as dialect mixing, code-switching, knowledge-intensive content, noisy environments, and multi-speaker conversations. Therefore, we present \u003cstrong\u003eMiMo-V2.5-ASR\u003c/strong\u003e, an end-to-end speech recognition model developed by the Xiaomi MiMo team. Through large-scale mid-training, high-quality supervised fine-tuning, and a novel reinforcement-learning algorithm, MiMo-V2.5-ASR achieves systematic improvements along the following dimensions:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🗣️ \u003cstrong\u003eChinese Dialects\u003c/strong\u003e: Native support for Wu, Cantonese, Hokkien, Sichuanese, and more.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🔀 \u003cstrong\u003eCode-Switch\u003c/strong\u003e: Seamless Chinese–English code-switching transcription with no language tags required.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🎵 \u003cstrong\u003eSong Recognition\u003c/strong\u003e: High-precision lyrics transcription for Chinese and English songs, even with mixed accompaniment and vocals.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🔊 \u003cstrong\u003eNoisy Environments\u003c/strong\u003e: Robust recognition under heavy noise, far-field capture, and other adverse acoustic conditions.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e👥 \u003cstrong\u003eMulti-Speaker\u003c/strong\u003e: Accurate transcription of overlapping, multi-party conversations such as meetings.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🇬🇧 \u003cstrong\u003eComplex English Scenarios\u003c/strong\u003e: Leading performance on the Open ASR Leaderboard for challenging English benchmarks such as AMI.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e📚 \u003cstrong\u003eKnowledge-Intensive Recognition\u003c/strong\u003e: Precise recognition of classical poetry, technical terminology, personal names, place names, and other knowledge-dense material.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e📝 \u003cstrong\u003eNative Punctuation\u003c/strong\u003e: Punctuation generated natively from prosody and semantics, delivering ready-to-use transcripts with no post-processing needed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Introduction</h2><a id="user-content-introduction" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Introduction" href="#introduction"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>MiMo-V2.5-ASR</strong> is a state-of-the-art end-to-end automatic speech recognition (ASR) model developed by the Xiaomi MiMo team. It is built to deliver accurate and robust transcription across Mandarin Chinese and English, multiple Chinese dialects, code-switched speech, song lyrics, knowledge-intensive content, noisy acoustic environments, and multi-speaker conversations. MiMo-V2.5-ASR achieves state-of-the-art results on a wide range of public benchmarks.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Abstract</h2><a id="user-content-abstract" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Abstract" href="#abstract"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Automatic speech recognition systems are expected to faithfully transcribe speech signals that originate from diverse languages, dialects, accents, and domains, and that are captured under a wide variety of acoustic conditions. While conventional end-to-end models perform well on in-domain data, they still fall short of real-world requirements in challenging scenarios such as dialect mixing, code-switching, knowledge-intensive content, noisy environments, and multi-speaker conversations. Therefore, we present <strong>MiMo-V2.5-ASR</strong>, an end-to-end speech recognition model developed by the Xiaomi MiMo team. Through large-scale mid-training, high-quality supervised fine-tuning, and a novel reinforcement-learning algorithm, MiMo-V2.5-ASR achieves systematic improvements along the following dimensions:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>🗣️ <strong>Chinese Dialects</strong>: Native support for Wu, Cantonese, Hokkien, Sichuanese, and more.</li>
<li>🔀 <strong>Code-Switch</strong>: Seamless Chinese–English code-switching transcription with no language tags required.</li>
<li>🎵 <strong>Song Recognition</strong>: High-precision lyrics transcription for Chinese and English songs, even with mixed accompaniment and vocals.</li>
<li>🔊 <strong>Noisy Environments</strong>: Robust recognition under heavy noise, far-field capture, and other adverse acoustic conditions.</li>
<li>👥 <strong>Multi-Speaker</strong>: Accurate transcription of overlapping, multi-party conversations such as meetings.</li>
<li>🇬🇧 <strong>Complex English Scenarios</strong>: Leading performance on the Open ASR Leaderboard for challenging English benchmarks such as AMI.</li>
<li>📚 <strong>Knowledge-Intensive Recognition</strong>: Precise recognition of classical poetry, technical terminology, personal names, place names, and other knowledge-dense material.</li>
<li>📝 <strong>Native Punctuation</strong>: Punctuation generated natively from prosody and semantics, delivering ready-to-use transcripts with no post-processing needed.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Results</h2><a id="user-content-results" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Results" href="#results"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">MiMo-V2.5-ASR has been evaluated across a broad set of benchmarks spanning standard Mandarin and English, Chinese dialects, lyric recognition, and internal business scenarios. The chart below summarizes the average performance of MiMo-V2.5-ASR across these scenarios.</p>

<p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="/XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-ASR/blob/main/assets/MiMo_ASR_Demo.png"><img src="/XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-ASR/raw/main/assets/MiMo_ASR_Demo.png" alt="MiMo-V2.5-ASR Demo" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto">This launches a local Gradio interface for MiMo-V2.5-ASR. You can:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Upload an audio file <strong>or</strong> record directly from your microphone.</li>
<li>Optionally specify a <strong>language tag</strong> (Chinese / English / Auto) to bias the model for a specific language, or leave it to <strong>Auto</strong> for automatic language detection (recommended for code-switched speech).</li>
<li>The demo calls the <code>asr_sft()</code> interface under the hood.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">To load the model and tokenizer automatically at startup, pass their paths on the command line:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>python run_mimo_asr.py \
    --model-path ./models/MiMo-V2.5-ASR \
    --tokenizer-path ./models/MiMo-Audio-Tokenizer</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="python run_mimo_asr.py \
    --model-path ./models/MiMo-V2.5-ASR \
    --tokenizer-path ./models/MiMo-Audio-Tokenizer" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>
</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>
  </div></div>
<p dir="auto">Otherwise, enter the local paths for <code>MiMo-Audio-Tokenizer</code> and <code>MiMo-V2.5-ASR</code> in the <strong>Model Configuration</strong> tab, then start transcribing!</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Python API</h2><a id="user-content-python-api" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Python API" href="#python-api"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Basic usage with the <code>asr_sft</code> interface:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">src</span>.<span class="pl-s1">mimo_audio</span>.<span class="pl-s1">mimo_audio</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-v">MimoAudio</span>

<span class="pl-s1">model</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">MimoAudio</span>(
    <span class="pl-s1">model_path</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"./models/MiMo-V2.5-ASR"</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">tokenizer_path</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"./models/MiMo-Audio-Tokenizer"</span>,
)

<span class="pl-c"># Automatic language detection (recommended for code-switching)</span>
<span class="pl-s1">text</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">model</span>.<span class="pl-c1">asr_sft</span>(<span class="pl-s">"path/to/audio.wav"</span>)
<span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s1">text</span>)

<span class="pl-c"># With explicit language tag</span>
<span class="pl-s1">text_zh</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">model</span>.<span class="pl-c1">asr_sft</span>(<span class="pl-s">"path/to/audio.wav"</span>, <span class="pl-s1">audio_tag</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"<chinese>"</span>)
<span class="pl-s1">text_en</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">model</span>.<span class="pl-c1">asr_sft</span>(<span class="pl-s">"path/to/audio.wav"</span>, <span class="pl-s1">audio_tag</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"<english>"</span>)</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="from src.mimo_audio.mimo_audio import MimoAudio

model = MimoAudio(
    model_path="./models/MiMo-V2.5-ASR",
    tokenizer_path="./models/MiMo-Audio-Tokenizer",
)

# Automatic language detection (recommended for code-switching)
text = model.asr_sft("path/to/audio.wav")
print(text)

# With explicit language tag
text_zh = model.asr_sft("path/to/audio.wav", audio_tag="<chinese>")
text_en = model.asr_sft("path/to/audio.wav", audio_tag="<english>")" tabindex="0" role="button">
